does: This paper documents OpenAI's approach to developing and deploying advanced AI systems safely, establishing concrete practices and frameworks that address the challenges of creating increasingly capable models. It describes safety processes integrated throughout the development lifecycle, from initial training design through red-teaming and external auditing to post-deployment monitoring. The work demonstrates how safety can be operationalized at scale across multiple stakeholders and systems, moving beyond theoretical discussions to implementation details of alignment techniques, evaluation protocols, and governance structures that aim to reduce risks from capable AI systems.
connects: This work builds on the growing field of AI safety and alignment research that has expanded from academic conceptualization to industry implementation. It extends earlier research on techniques like reinforcement learning from human feedback, adversarial testing, and interpretability work by embedding them into production systems and organizational processes. The paper connects to broader governance and risk-management literature while responding to the increasing urgency around developing safe approaches to training large language models, informed by concerns raised in the AI safety community about misalignment, misuse, and unintended harms from powerful systems.
enables: This operational framework enables future safety research by providing concrete implementation examples and data that can inform iterative improvements in AI safety practices across the industry. It establishes baseline standards and transparency about safety processes that can encourage broader adoption of responsible development practices, potentially setting norms for how organizations approach AGI development. The work points toward deeper questions about evaluating and assuring safety guarantees as systems become more capable, and creates infrastructure for ongoing research into new safety challenges that emerge with more advanced models and broader deployment scenarios.
